DECLARATION OF EMMANUEL PUN IN SUPPORT OF MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO REMOVE OR REDUCE MECHANIC'S LIEN FILED BY CROSS COMPLAINANT MEAKIN, HUGH AN INDIVIDUAL MEAKIN, MELBA AN INDIVIDUAL

LAW AND MOTION 302, ARGUED AND THE COURT MODIFIED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: DEFENDANTS HUGH MEAKIN, MELBA MEAKIN, SARAH MEAKIN AND PHILLIP MEAKIN'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO REMOVE MECHANIC'S LIEN IS DENIED. THE DECISION IN KIM V. JF ENTERPRISES (1996) 42 CAL. APP. 4TH 849, 856 ESTABLISHES THAT KNOWLEDGE OF A CONTRACTOR'S WORK PROVIDES A BASIS TO ESTOP THE OWNER FROM RELYING ON THE NOTICE REQUIREMENTS OF CC SECTION 3097. THE DECLARATIONS IN THIS CASE RAISE A QUESTION AS TO THE EXISTENCE OF A CONTRACT BETWEEN PLAINTIFF AND DEFENDANTS, BUT ESTABLISH THAT HUGH MEAKIN HAD KNOWLEDGE OF THE WORK. THE COURT GRANTED THE REQUEST TO REDUCE THE MECHANIC'S LIEN BY $22,750.00. PREVAILING PARTY TO PREPARE A FORM OF ORDER. JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600

Notice Of Motion And Motion For An Order To Remove Or Reduce Mechanic'S Lien
Off Calendar Oct-28-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances:
David Tillotson of Janin Morgan & Brenner (510.379.1717) for plaintiff and responding party
Daron Tong of McKague and Tong (409.5222) for defendants and moving parties

Argued and the Court modified its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS HUGH MEAKIN, MELBA MEAKIN, SARAH MEAKIN AND PHILLIP MEAKIN'S MOTION FOR AN ORDER TO REMOVE MECHANIC'S LIEN is denied. The decision in Kim v. JF Enterprises (1996) 42 Cal. App. 4th 849, 856 establishes that knowledge of a contractor's work provides a basis to estop the owner from relying on the notice requirements of CC section 3097. The declarations in this case raise a question as to the existence of a contract between plaintiff and defendants, but establish that Hugh Meakin had knowledge of the work. The Court granted the request to reduce the mechanic's lien by $22,750.00. Prevailing party to prepare a form of order. (302/PJM/PB) Judge: Patrick J. Mahoney, Reporter: Maria Torreano, CSR 8600
